reviews.text:
Horrible experience with the Arrasmith Firm. We hired the Arasmith Firm for a straightforward and routine, but time sensitive legal matter. After paying our retainer and having a reasonable intake process, we gained almost nothing further of any substance from the team during the weeks we were under their care. We reached out multiple times by text, phone and email for updates and to ask questions and got no substantive responses. We were charged for several of these phone calls, despite the fact we continued to learn nothing except things like - "the lawyers were super busy". And ofcourse, the phone calls only took place because our other requests for information had not been successful. We finally gave the team a deadline to provide an update and answer some incredibly basic questions about our case, (like who files paperwork?). No response was received and thus we terminated our relationship and sought a refund for our retainer.
The Arrasmith team then refused to share any information about what had been billed on our retainer for 30 days. In our opinion, this was in retribution for terminating our relationship as the Arrasmith Attorney Client contract clearly states: #10: "Clients may request a refund at any time and the Attorney will keep client fully informed regarding the status and amount of any such refund".
When we were finally provided our invoice, the firm had the audacity to claim that we actually owed them money, despite the fact that in our opinion we had not had any evidence of significant progress on our case. We strongly feel the invoice was riddled with exaggerations and misrepresentations. Perhaps the most outrageous line item expense on this invoice was a charge for 600$ for a discussion that Mr Arasmith had with his team to castigate them for how badly they mismanaged our case. In this line item, Mr Arrasmith details how poorly we were treated, and apparently for a full hour reprimanded and remediated his team at my expense...... billing me $600.00 for the conversation they all had about what a horrible job they did on my case.  And the best part is, the discussion this expense references took place AFTER I had already terminated our relationship with the Arrasmith firm.
Furthermore, the afternoon after we terminated our relationship, Mr Arrasmith sent us a 6 page document, which amounted to what one of our attorney friends described as "overkill" for our incredibly routine and simple case telling us information that was either obvious, easily found online, or that we already knew (we are investigating how much if any of this document was AI generated). Mr Arrasmith, predictably billed us excessively for this, despite the fact that we received it well after terminating our relationship. Mr Arrasmith responded that the work done on this document was before we terminated the relationship and that it had been sent by email earlier. We ofcourse never received any other email, and he provided no proof that an earlier email had been sent. Moreover, I had my IT guy look at the pdf. The metadata associated with this pdf clearly shows it was created the afternoon after we had terminated our relationship with the firm. Furthermore, we could see that the software that produced the pdf was a Mac OS/MS compatible software that allowed other programs such as word processing programs including Microsoft Word, to 'save as pdf'. Essentially this demonstrates that Mr Arrasmith was 'creating' and working on this document up until the moment he sent it to us, well after we terminated our relationship. This is a significant misrepresentation and we are investigating further with the CA Bar and our current attorney exactly how bad this is.
As mentioned we're submitting complaints to the CA Bar and the Better Business Bureau, and have sought the counsel of another attorney, who basically in a single day, for far less money, answered our questions and provided us the services we required.

The Law Offices of James L. Arrasmith helped me through a very difficult family law situation with so much care and professionalism. They listened, guided me, and always made me feel supported. I’m truly grateful for everything they did.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Berna. I'm grateful for your kind words and glad our team could offer the care, attentive listening, and guidance that supported you through a difficult time.

reviews.text:
DO NOT RECOMMEND.

Zero transparency as to what will be invoice. I never once received any indication of what work would be done prior to being invoiced. Retainer paid on 09/03. Termination on 10/06. Total Cost $3,420 for 5 days where I received 0 documentation, just a questionnaire that I never answered.

I hired James L Arrasmith to assist on a case that involved wrongful eviction and domestic partnership on 09/29.   Prior to this I paid $240 for a 30 minute consultation that was rescheduled multiple times because the attorney was not available. No where near the scheduled time and at my place of work, Arrasmith calls and starts the consultation. The call was very disorganized but I was given assurance that I have a case to be won.

I paid the $5,000 retainer and by 09/30 I received a follow-up questionnaire by associate at the firm. I DID NOT respond to the questionnaire as it required a lot of bandwidth on my part to gather the information needed to answer the questions (billing statements, messages, photos, etc.). In the process of gathering the information and developing answers to the questionnaire, I decided to go a different route for how I would proceed with the case.

10/04 I received an invoice totaling $990 for work leading up to that date.

10/06 I sent a letter of termination of representation.  (I receive 0 acknowledgment that this has been received).

10/24 I am given "Conclusion of Legal Services".

10/25-10/28 - I make multiple calls to check in on the refund of my retainer and all documentation associated with my case. I am told that I will receive final invoicing 30 days after the letter of termination was submitted.   11/06 - I receive final invoice that includes that initial $990 invoice from 10/04, but now an additional invoice from 10/01 that is for 4 hours of work totaling to $2,430. The itemized description included nothing of value beyond the information that was provided on the consultation call! It noted "Comprehensive review" "Analysis" "detailed legal research" "documented legal basis" "prepared comprehensive legal memorandum" - nothing was provided or justifies the 4 hours of work that just reiterates the information from the consultation call.

Again, I did not respond to the questionnaire. I did not provide text messages, photos or anything of the such that would require a deep analysis to which this was billed.

What makes it more interesting is that per the agreement, should the account fall below 40% of the retainer amount, the client is required to replenish the account to the full amount.   By this math my account was below this threshold on 10/01, and no one in the office called to tell me that my account needed replenishment for 7 days.

Completely unethical and I will move forward with filings with the California State Bar, BBB, and consumer fraud units.

reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you so much, Sara. We sincerely appreciate your five-star rating; we're delighted to hear you were satisfied. We would be happy to serve you again whenever you wish.

reviews.text: I strongly recommend that anyone reading this review NOT hire the James Arrasmith Law Firm to Represent them. I gave them 1 star only because zero stars is not possible. We signed a Contract for them to do three things for me: 1.  File an Answer to a Quiet Title Complaint, 2.  File a Cross-Complaint,  3.  File a Lis Pendens to block anyone from selling the property in question, and is required to be filed in a Quiet Title Action. I got concerned when I sent them my Responses to the issues Raised in the Quiet Title Action, and their 'Answer' consisted of them transposing my Responses directly onto paper as the Answer, they barely added anything, and yes, I paid them Thousands of dollars to copy my Responses word-for-word and filing it as the Answer. The Cross-complaint was required to be filed at the same time as the Answer, and they failed to properly formatted the Caption of all things, and that got the Cross-Complaint rejected by the Court. They 'fixed' the caption and refiled the next day - a day late, and that was rejected too. Then told me after repeated phone calls they just never answered, they'd now have to file multiple motions to the Judge and ask for permission to file a late cross-complaint. Of course, they charged me thousands of dollars more while they worked on getting the Judge to accept a late filing. On my own, I found a obscure Court Rule, that would allow the Cross Complaint to be filed late, but deemed filed on time , for a minor formatting issue(such as a improper caption) They declined to pursue that avenue, and when I addressed the Rule to the Court myself and got them to agree to accept the Cross-Complaint as timely filed, they refused to do it, and I was forced to do it myself. The thousands of dollars in charges they racked up to correct the flawed Cross-Complaint and preparations to ask the Judge to file a late Cross-Complaint, they still billed me for and took out of my Retainer. When opposing parties reached out to them to 'Meet & Confer' as required by the Court Rules, they simply ignored them. We were under a Limited Representation Contract, to do 3 things, they never even attempted to file the Lis Pendens, a simple, closed document, that they'd not be able to gouge me further on, so they simply did not file one at all. Breach of Contract. They failed to file with the Court the Required form that showed every party involved in the Case that they were only doing 3 things. As more parties reached out to conduct the required 'Meet & Confer' , they started to grant parties "Waivers" extending the time the Parties had to Respond, they had no authority to do so and then failed to notify the Court as required by Court Rule that they were doing so - if they had the authority to do so at all. As timeframes ran out, Entries of Default were filed with the Court, blocking Parties from participating in the Quiet Title Action after that. Then approximately 5 days before the Demurrer Hearing they Started telling the Opposing Parties - THEY WERE NO LONGER REPRESENTING ME. They did not Notify me at all, I only found out when the Opposing Parties contacted me and told me the James Arrasmith Law Firm told them that they were no longer representing me. Days before a critical hearing, and left me high & dry trying to find another attorney to represent me at the last minute. The Law Office of James Arrasmith, siphoned over $21,000.00 from the Retainers I covered at their demand, for that 21 grand, I got a Answer that was just my own Responses to THEM based on the Quiet Title Complaint, a Cross-Complaint that was never properly completed and we're still trying to recover from that failure, and then they never fulfilled the terms of the Contract at all, never filed a Lis Pendens. A clear Breach of Contract. I advise anyone reading this to keep looking for another attorney, they will do you like they did me, and leave you worse off than when you started. I am in the process of formulating a ethics complaint which you'll be able to see on the calbar website shortly. Good Luck. God Bless.

I for one came to the Arrasmith group with rose colored glasses. That is to say, I had already been turned down by at least a dozen lawyers as they saw no merit or profitability in my case suing a former attourney for malpractice. Arrasmith stated I must act fast so the statute time doesn’t run out. I was convinced my case was rock solid so I hired them. I sent the necessary retainer $5k and spoke with an actual attourney. He was a brilliant thinker which impressed me but his accent was so thick I had to ask to repeat everything during our 45 minute interview. I’m thinking great this is what I’m paying $300/hr for, but wearing my roses glasses I pressed on. That was my only contact with a lawyer, till the last.
I was kept updated by “the company press secretary” at $145/hr. They wrote a demand letter but could not find who to send it to. They sent to a defunct address in Oakland and to someplace in Florida, what Florida? Why. I did a simple white pages internet search and found an address no problem that was concluded to be correct. They sent it but yielded no response. They requested another retainer for a little more than the first. To persue court activity. All the while I maintained contact with press secretary and more often with accountants to consider contingency and payment arrangements. After a few stalled payments and failed contingency talks they concluded that my case was not persuable. I spoke to another attorney at this point as they kept reassigning them elsewhere, a total of three, I only spoke with two. She concluded there was no faith in the case. It took them $13k to come to this conclusion where everybody else did it for free.

We were only able to get a consultation, but the senior attorney, Michael Benavides, Esq. was very helpful in answering our questions and concerns. Our questions pertained to a dog being deemed dangerous by Animal Control. He gave good legal advice to carry with us in our upcoming hearing. We spent about 40 minutes over the phone, and he did not make us feel rushed or pressed with the amount of time we could spend with him.
reviews.ownerResponse: Ariel, thank you for the kind review. I’m glad Michael provided helpful, unhurried guidance—please reach out if you need further assistance before your hearing. Wishing you the best.

Professional,  knowledgeable about my concerns,  going forward.

reviews.text: I needed legal assistance evicting a tenant. I found this firm using a Google search and saw that the Google reviews were positive. I reached out for an initial consultation and provided all of the relevant facts. Based on these facts, I was provided with an estimate for drafting a three day notice to quit (for eviction), serving the notice and filing for eviction proceedings with the court. I was told that the actual costs from the estimate could vary a bit and if the firm’s appearance in court was required, this could cost more. This seemed pretty standard, so I immediately paid the estimated cost as a retainer so we could move forward. The firm requested I write down the relevant facts that had been discussed during our consultation, which I also provided promptly. After this, I heard nothing from the firm for weeks. I started calling and emailing to request updates. Responses received were generally lacking in detail and reflected very little progress. I threatened to terminate the relationship and only then was I provided with a draft of the three day notice to quit which was a pretty standard three day notice to quit with some of the written facts that I had provided incorporated. I immediately reviewed the draft of the three day notice when received and asked when it could be finalized and served. About this time I also received an invoice detailing charges for a little less than half of the original retainer / estimate. While I felt like that was a lot for just the draft three day notice that had been provided, I was eager to just get it finally served to the tenant as it had been over 6 weeks since I’d originally engaged the firm at this point. But again, despite calls and emails, the firm was not providing any updates. Frustrated with a lack of progress, I provided notification that I was terminating the firm’s services. A few days later, I received another invoice that essentially used up the remainder of the original retainer / estimate. I understand that the original estimate / retainer could vary based on actuals, but I do not understand how the firm managed to blow through the entire retainer when they only drafted a pretty standard letter. There was no serving and no filing eviction documents with the court. I am left to wonder if the firm provided a low-ball estimate up-front just to hook my business, or did they inflate actual costs after I terminated service in order to avoid providing a refund of the remaining unused retainer? I reached out to the firm to review their billing and they insist it is accurate. Upon research, I have found reviews that suggest I am not the only customer that has had these types of issues with this firm. I urge you to do your homework before engaging with this firm.

Very helpful, knowledge of the process and explained the process and how the law works in the field of my issue. It takes both money and time , and evidence . Proof , PROOF , PROOF!!  I have the proof, just not the money yet .
reviews.ownerResponse:

reviews.text:
Beware of The Law Offices of James L. Arrasmith
I hired this firm to represent me in an arbitration case against Kaiser for a permanent heart injury. I paid a $5,000 retainer, which was rapidly consumed, and then I was billed until charges exceeded $10,400. Despite these payments, the firm never obtained a medical expert, never represented me in arbitration, and provided no evidence of meaningful work performed—only invoices.
They mishandled my case by choosing only one arbitrator when my Kaiser arbitration required a panel of three. This fundamental error alone severely damaged my case, stripping me of the fair and balanced review I was entitled to. On top of that, they filed unauthorized actions and produced nothing that advanced my claim.
In my opinion, they clearly had no idea how to represent someone in arbitration and instead used people who are not lawyers to do all the work. These individuals seemed to know nothing about the process and failed to provide any competent representation. James L. Arrasmith advertises himself as specializing in a wide range of areas of law—landlord and tenant law, eviction law, personal injury, family law, estate planning, immigration, and many others—but in my experience, he gives the impression of not knowing anything at all. Everything I saw showed unprofessionalism and substandard work. I also believe much of what was provided to me was generated using ChatGPT, rather than true legal expertise. In my opinion, none of them, lawyers included, acted professionally.
Their behavior gives me a strong impression of fraud. I have made a police report and am disputing the charges with my bank in order to seek a refund. Despite all this, the firm continues to demand an additional $3,135 for their baseless invoices.
Adding to the concern, it appears to me that all of the five-star reviews online are manipulated, while the overwhelming majority of authentic reviews are one-star accounts from clients reporting the same pattern: excessive billing, large retainers, no actual representation, and hostile communication once services are questioned. My personal experience matches that pattern.
In my opinion, consumers should exercise extreme caution before hiring this firm.

From start to finish my case was mishandled. I was given false information from a member of their team that ultimately led to my mediation for custody to be useless. I was unable to provide my evidence. Prior to the court date I was told they were going to talk my my ex lawyer and would call me to discuss the results. I was never called. So on the day of court I was blind sided. I voiced by frustrations and was given no answers to why.
I felt completely lost dealing with defamation online. They explained my options and helped me clear my name and protect my reputation.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for your kind review, Kassahun. We are glad our team was able to help clarify your legal options and assist you in protecting your reputation. We truly appreciate you sharing your experience with The Law Offices of James L. Arrasmith.

I highly recommend Bill Bowen, Bowen Law Firm. He and his assistant, Lori, stuck with me through a lengthy and very challenging case. They were always kind, patient, and ethical. This was a David and Goliath story and Bill never backed down against the large and powerful defendant in my case.

The defendant threw tens of thousands of pages of “discovery” at us. What’s that saying, if you can’t dazzle them with brilliance, baffle them with BS? Well, that’s what the defendant did – tried to baffle us with BS. However, Bill and Lori were amazing in their ability to sort through, prioritize, and concisely summarize the documents.

The defendant’s attorney wasted many days of our time with her sloppy and aggressive deposition style. Quite the opposite, Bill’s deposition style was precise, professional, and effective. His focused and calm demeanor during deposition was reassuring.

Bill is approachable and easy to talk to. He was honest with me about the merits of my case. When he was billing me hourly, his charges were fair. That was really important to me because the previous attorney on my case charged me a lot, did nothing for me, and then dropped my case, leaving me desperate for help.

My case took years and Bill stuck it out with me. I’m very grateful for that. Not many lawyers wanted to take on the defendant in my case.

reviews.text: Just like most, circumstances and life put me in a situation I didn’t know how to navigate. Hoss and his team are not only professional, but also very thoughtful, and caring while working with their clientele (me).  Legal paperwork and processes are quite overwhelming to me, especially amidst all the responsibilities, emotions and grief I was experiencing at the moment.  Hoss and team were able to help simplify and guide me to what and how I needed to do things with minimum issues.  The results of my cases ended in a very favorable outcome for me and the children, but I also feel it was fair to my ex as well.  As my first experience with a lawyer and our legal system, I couldn’t have asked for a better one.  My family and I send all our love to Hoss, his family, and his team.  We are and continue to be thankful and grateful for all of the sacrifice, efforts, and care he and his team made for us.

reviews.text:
Gave their office a call 3 times today before a live person answered the phone. When one finally did, I asked for an attorney to review a will for a large family estate and the lady I spoke with was INCREDIBLY rude and dismissive. She asked if there was a dispute, to which I clearly stated there was not. I tried to provide more detail but she dismissed me entirely and said I needed a litigation attorney, not an estate attorney. Shen then told me to call back MetLife by which I was referred, tell them it’s a disputed will, then call Wade Law again and ask for the right type of lawyer.

How does a serious law firm have someone like that answering their phone? I hope they record their calls because that lady should be fired.

Lastly, based on additional research and reviews, it appears Wade Law is attempting to manipulate the MetLife legal services program for larger litigation fees while rendering basic estate services.

I’m in no way going to be party to Wade Law gaming MetLife’s benefit program and I will be reporting my experience to their quality team.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Aaron, I'm sorry to hear about your experience trying to schedule an appointment. We regulary work on ensuring that our receptionists answer calls in a timely and professional manner. I apologize if that wasn't the case for you.

Because we aren't taking new litigation cases currently, our receptionists will usually try and confirm whether or not the case might end up going to ligitation. And if so, they will usually recommend that the client contact an attorney that is taking litigation cases so that the client doesn't have to start over again with someone new if that ends up being the case.

We are certainly not trying to game Metlife's benefit program for larger ligitation fees. Metlife's fees are fixed. And as I mentioned, we aren't taking new litigation cases currently. In addition, Metlife's coverage doesn't extend to Will disputes. So there would be no way for us to try and game their system, even if we wanted to.

If you would like to discuss this matter further, please feel free to contact our office and speak to David Wade.
Thank you for the 5 stars, Margaret!
Thanks so much for your 5 star review!

My mom recommended Jeffrey Davis and I am so thankful I gave him a call. He immediately eased my concerns and walked me through what steps I needed to take.

Jeffrey and his team took care of the entire process and fought for me. I’m so thankful I didn’t have to spend months of my time hounding insurance companies for reimbursement. His help allowed me to focus on recovering and getting back to normal.

If you’re ever in a situation like this and feel overwhelmed, please give Jeffrey a call. You’ll be well taken care of.

reviews.text:
This is the 2nd time I retained Jeff Davis. I chose him to represent me because he and Kerry treated me with compassion and respect. I felt like they actually cared about me and the outcome of my case. Unfortunately, I was involved in a very serious motorcycle accident that lead to a lengthy process, but through it all, Jeff and Kerry kept me up to date and informed with what was transpiring.  Throughout the process, there were times I felt hopeless, and just wanted to give up the battle. Jeff and Kerry encouraged me to seek out other medical professions, and keep my head up. They made sure that I always knew that their number one concern was for me to make a full recovery and getting the best care possible. Literally, when I felt there was no hope, they didn't ever allow me to give up. I know from first hand experience, that when you are going through such a life changing catastrophic event, there is no other attorney i would rather have representing me. If you are looking for representation that will help ease you through what is probably a very difficult situation,  look no further than Jeff Davis' office.
Thank you Davis Law Firm!
Sincerely,
John Bane
reviews.ownerResponse:

John, it is truly an honor to work with and assist people such as yourself. Best wishes.
Jeffery Davis, Kerry and staff.

In addition caused my SDI claim to lapse, failed to inform me that I received a loan from the insurance company, failed to submit my mileage and reimbursements claim, and failed to get me the correct documents needed to continue with my MediCal and Social Security General Assistance plan. The excuse? "Confusion"

ALL documents are filed electronically, there are no court appearances, no personal service processors, no Zoom conferences, nothing. It's all done remotely.

I was told I could get $46k plus the retraining voucher, I got $18k and no voucher.
I am in a worse position now than I was when I started with this firm.

reviews.text:
From our first meeting, Dustin made what can feel like an overwhelming process very manageable. Estate planning is not something most people deal with often, and there are a lot of moving parts. He took the time to explain how a revocable living trust works, how it helps avoid probate, and why proper funding of the trust is just as important as drafting the documents themselves. He walked us through trustee selection, successor trustees, distributions, and how to think through allocations in a way that protects both assets and relationships.

He also helped us think carefully about medical directives, powers of attorney, and incapacity planning. These are not easy conversations, but Dustin approached them with clarity and professionalism. He made sure we understood not just what we were signing, but why it mattered and how it would function in real life.

One thing we appreciated most was his practical insight. He offered guidance on how to structure the trust in a way that supports our family long term, including considerations for future changes, potential tax implications, and how to avoid common mistakes that can create confusion later. He answered questions patiently and never rushed the discussion.

The process was organized, timely, and efficient. We always knew the next step and felt confident that everything was being handled properly. By the end, we had a fully executed and funded trust, updated directives, and clear instructions for our trustees.

More than anything, we now have peace of mind. We know our affairs are in order and that our family will be taken care of now and well into the future. We are grateful for Dustin’s guidance and would confidently recommend California Probate and Trust, PC to anyone looking for thoughtful and trustworthy estate planning support.

They are professional and personal.
We are very satisfied.
Rude and insulting
reviews.ownerResponse:
If you are shopping for a trust, and reading this, hello. It is true we can't make everyone happy. We have standards. We know we are not the right fit for everyone. Theresa and I spoke yesterday. She was lovely and delightful. But she decided that she lived too far away from my office. Additionally, she was calling to get a price. I was very upfront and told her we don't quote pricing over the phone, and that we're not the cheapest.

In nearly every call like Theresa's, I advise that there are many DIY options, and there are even some non lawyers selling trust services for not much money. I explain that my firm spends a lot of time with clients, we dig into their goals and objectives. We learn about family issues that could create problems down the road. We do what online services can't, and what cheap providers won't. We invest a lot of time asking questions and more questions so we can create tailored solutions for your family.

Having standards is great. Our clients are fantastic. We get to pick them. Admittedly, we don't work with everyone. First, we can't. And second, sometimes people have personality differences or expectations that are not achievable. It's great to be able to be selective. I want to enjoy my time with clients, and I want clients to enjoy their time with me.

I hope Theresa can find what she is looking for. I know there are 1,000,000 options for her, and there is a solution that will meet her needs. But this time, we are not that solution. If I was rude or insulting, I am sorry. That was not my intention. My hope was to encourage Theresa to continue searching for a solution that fits her needs.

reviews.text:
BE VERY AWARE IF YOU USE THIS FIRM!!!

My mom who is 85 years old already had her trust written. She just wanted Ca Probate and Trust, PC  to rewrite her trust and add a few more items. She paid the exorbitant price of $4,900.
They scheduled 3 MEETINGS for her:
1)The "Design meeting" where she met her attorney Ben and stated what she wanted.
2)The "Review meeting" where Ben showed her the trust he had prepared for her by pulling up info on a large screen.
3)The "Signing meeting" where she was to go in and sign her trust.

After her attorney, Ben, pulled my mom's important trust information up on a large screen during her review meeting, he told her she didn't really need to read the rest because it was just basic trust info. No one, especially an attorney, should advise NOT to read everything before signing!!! VERY SUSPECT!!

A few days before going in to sign her trust, she read through what Ben had written up. She discovered the owner of CA Probate and Trust, PC, Dustin MacFarlane, was in her trust as a "Trust Protector Appointer" without her knowledge or permission!!

It gives this company an opportunity to charge more fees when you become incapacitated or deceased. AND even worse, it gives all kinds of powers to the "Trust Protector" of Dustin's choosing, like the ability to fire your children as trustees!!!

Dustin will try to explain it away by saying his name is just a "place holder", but if my mom had listened to their attorney, Ben, when he told her she didn't need to read the rest before signing, Dustin would have remained in my mom's trust without her knowledge or consent!

Also, I am not disputing the idea of a "trust protector" or "trust protector appointer",  but we are worried they are slipping this into people's trusts without their knowledge so Dustin can charge fees when the person becomes incapacitated or deceased. BBB will investigate and find out if this concern of ours is true.

My mom is taking them to small claims to get her $4,900 back because she clearly no longer trusts them.

In order to protect other elderly people who are using this company, she is filing claims with the Better Business Bureau and the Ca Bar Association.  Hopefully their investigations will get to the bottom of this!!

Clearly, WE RECOMMEND YOU USE A DIFFERENT LAW FIRM FOR YOUR TRUST or...

My mom has used Harry E. Feiler for nearly a decade. He's inexpensive and thorough. She is sticking with him and is no longer using an attorney. He's excellent, experienced and HONEST!! He also only charges $1,200 and his trusts are GREAT! He is a LEGAL DOCUMENT ASSISTANT AND NOT AN ATTORNEY. My mom only decided to get an attorney involved because she was worried people she disinherited would try and sue upon her death. But that is when we would hire an attorney, beforehand turned out to be unnecessary.

ps if you read my other reviews over the years, you will find they are all positive. I am writing this because I REALLY WANT PEOPLE TO BE AWARE BEFORE THEY USE THIS COMPANY!!

pss Dan Z in a yahoo review stated they offer gift cards to people who write them 5 star reviews, so take that in to consideration as well!

Dustin's defense is stating my mom "didn't understand" MY MOM UNDERSTOOD PERFECTLY AND HAS HAD TRUSTS FOR DECADES WRITTEN BY BOTH ATTORNEYS AND LEGAL DOCUMENT ASSISTANTS.
OF COURSE SHE DIDN'T WANT TO LISTEN TO DUSTIN TRY AND EXPLAIN HIS WAY OUT OF THE FACT THAT HIS FIRM RECOMMENDED SHE NOT READ THE REST OF WHAT THEY HAD PREPARED BEFORE SHE SIGNED AND THEN FINDING HE HAD HIS NAME ADDED TO HER TRUST WITHOUT HER PERMISSION!

GIVE ME A BREAK!
Honest, hardworking, and great at what they do. Such a big fan of this firm!

I RECOMMEND YOU DO NOT USE THIS FIRM!!!

1) Benjamin V. Platon pulled up my trust information in my LAST review meeting before signing AND THEN TOLD ME I REALLY DIDN'T NEED TO READ THE REST OF MY TRUST BECAUSE IT WAS JUST BOILERPLATE TRUST INFO.

2) I did read my trust completely a few days before going in to their office to sign it and discovered THE OWNER OF THIS FIRM, DUSTIN MACFARLANE, WAS IN MY TRUST AS A "TRUST PROTECTOR APPOINTER" WITHOUT ME BEING ASKED OR INFORMED!! 😡

It gives them the opportunity to charge more money for their services when you become incapacitated or deceased.
The trust protector of Dustin's choosing had the authority to fire my children as trustees among other shocking rights!!

It was fishy enough hearing an attorney tell me I didn't need to read my trust completely before signing, but discovering Dustin's name on my trust without my permission or knowledge is beyond suspect!! Also, I already had my trust written up before using their service. They were only to add a few items.

I think if people do discover his name in their trusts,  Dustin tells them "it's just a place holder". But had I not discovered this, Dustin, who I did NOT like, would have had powers in my trust that I did NOT authorize!!

I am taking them to small claims to get my $4,900 returned.

I am filing this information with the Better Business Bureau and the Ca Bar Association in order to protect other elderly people (I'm 85) like myself.

In Dustin's response he wrote, "This is why it’s crucial to read the trust document and ask questions." EXACTLY, WHICH IS WHY THE ATTORNEY FROM CA PROBATE AND TRUST, BEN, TELLING ME I DIDN'T NEED TO READ THE REST OF MY TRUST AFTER SUPPOSEDLY SHOWING ME EVERYTHING ON A LARGE SCREEN SEEMS UNETHICAL.
Dustin rambling on about Trust Protectors and Trust Protector Appointers has absolutely NOTHING TO DO WITH THE FACT THAT HE PUT HIMSELF IN MY TRUST AS A "TRUST PROTECTOR APPOINTER" WITHOUT MY KNOWLEDGE!!

I will leave it to the BBB investigation to discover if this is a long running pattern with this firm. Needless to say, I don't trust them and recommend to anyone wanting a trust to go elsewhere! I am sticking with Harry E. Feiler who has taken care of my trusts for nearly a decade. He is knowledgable, HONEST and inexpensive! He only charged me $1,000 for my last trust I had him write up. I only went to an attorney when I discovered people I had disinherited plan on contesting my trust once I have passed. It turns out my trustee just needs to hire an attorney once this happens. Dustin even said it would be a different attorney and not his firm once that took place in the future.
Dustin was kind and professional. Even though he did not end up needing to represent me, based on our conversation, I can tell he would be a friendly, knowledgeable, and easy to work with attorney.

reviews.text:
After working with another attorney and losing multiple court hearings on all fronts, I was emotionally drained and honestly losing hope. My divorce case was already in progress when I contacted Olga Koplik, and I am beyond grateful that she agreed to take it on. From day one, she gave me something I desperately needed — peace of mind. For the first time in a long while, I could actually sleep.

My case was complicated and full of stress. Every time I thought another motion might come up, my anxiety spiked — but Olga always found a way to work it out in our favor. She did it professionally, strategically, and almost like magic. In the courtroom, she is a true shark — fearless, sharp, and always two steps ahead. Outside the courtroom, she was calm, reassuring, and always there when I needed guidance, even in the evenings.

The results speak for themselves. I hadn’t seen my daughter for a while, and now I can see her whenever I want. Financially, what Olga achieved feels nothing short of a miracle. Throughout the entire process, I never once felt overcharged — not a single penny. She is fair, ethical, and completely transparent.

This is, without question, the best attorney I have ever worked with. She also speaks Russian, which was incredibly helpful. If anyone from the Russian-speaking community is looking for a top-notch, aggressive, and highly professional attorney — Olga Koplik is the one. My honest advice: trust her, listen to her, and let her do her work. You will be in very good hands.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Eugene. It was such a pleasuring working with you. I know this was not an easy road for you. Knowing that you and your daughter have fully reunited, is the best news anyone could ask for. I wish you both all the best.

reviews.text: Olga Koplik was professional, responsive, and easy to work with. She explained everything clearly and helped me through my divorce without unnecessary stress. I always felt supported and confident in her guidance. I would definitely recommend her.

reviews.text: Tried to potentially hire Olga to help my son with a family matter.  I called her office and let them know of my sons upcoming hearing in 2 weeks.  I was told my son could pay $300 for a consultation, but wouldn't know until we paid that whether or not Olga's calendar would be open and available on the date of the hearing.  Every other attorney we have called has been able to check their openings on their calendar without requiring a consultation fee.  I did not ask if Olga would represent my son, only if that date was open on her calendar.  Olga was very poorly represented by Emma in her office who answered the phone.  Emma apparently has no access to her boss' calendar.
reviews.ownerResponse: Mr. Lunsford, I am sorry for your experience. However, I believe there has been some confusion. What you asked my receptionist is whether I will be able to "represent" your son at his upcoming hearing. I understand that you were looking for a guarantee that someone would represent your son prior to spending money on a consultation, but without a consultation that is impossible to say. No attorney can commit to a case without knowing the facts and determine whether the issues are the type the attorney can help them with. I do not believe that it is fair for you to leave my firm a negative review when your inquiry was unreasonable. A consultation is intended for the attorney to answer questions and give advice. Scheduling a consultation does not automatically guarantee your representation. Please also note that if an attorney takes on a case and a scheduling conflict arises, there are always options for a continuance or alternatively an associate in the firm can appear in court as well. I wish you and your son all the best and hope you find the representation you need.

reviews.text:
I retained Ms. Koplik for my step-parent adoption. Ms.
Koplik went above and beyond to locate the biological parent so that we can
move my case forward. Ms. Koplik kept me updated and made sure that I was aware
of every step leading to the final adoption. My son is only 5 years old and he
has not had any contact with his mother since he was 3 months old. My son did
not know nor understand the biology behind parenting, thus I was very scared
and unsure how to break this news to him. Ms. Koplik set us up with a professional
counselor that guided my husband and I in how to gently tell our son about the
adoption, before the court’s investigator spoke to him and caused confusion. Ms.
Koplik gifted us kids books that further allowed us to educate and ease our son
into the adoption process. We are a complete family because of her.

When life places you to seek for legal guidance, it's confusing, scary and to say the least very stressful. A divorce process, can make you feel like you're in a dark room.

Her knowledge with the legal system is impeccable. She meticulously went through all previous paperwork that we had submitted and corrected the mess from previous direction from others. We walked in there stressed, confused and scared but walked out crying. Tears of Happiness! Human Kindness is something that is not as common as we would like for it to be, especially during these times in our lives. When you meet Olga A. Koplik...you meet Human Kindness. Choose someone to represent YOU... that cares for YOU. You will not regret this decision in your Life.
Thank you Olga!
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Jaime for your very kind words. This means the world to me. It was my pleasure helping your daughter and I am happy to hear that everything worked out and she was no longer given the runaround by the court clerks. She has been through so much and definitely deserved a break. I wish her and her children the best, and should you ever need anything else, you know where to find me. You are a great father and don’t ever stop helping your children the way you do. They are lucky to have you.

reviews.text:
“Discretion is the better part of valor.”

In navigating the complex path that lay before me when I embarked on a journey requiring a Family Law attorney, I needed to have few but very critical criteria met. My attorney needed to be: exceptionally knowledgeable in Family Law; comfortable and confident in the courtroom, level-headed and even-handed in negotiations, and most importantly - crystal clear about the outcome that I wanted to achieve.

Olga Koplik demonstrated that she is extremely well-versed in Family Law, which helped to inform the strategy for my matter. While I needed to accomplish my goals, it was important that we did so out of court, in order to keep family matters private. To this end, I trusted Olga to apply pressure when it was necessary and to compromise when it would yield the best outcome for all.

Olga is a remarkable blend of utmost professional and deeply caring. She advocated for me based on her superb command of jurisprudence and her compassion as a mother. She put pressure on opposing counsel in order to protect my family interests and the matter was expeditiously resolved out of court and to my great satisfaction.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Cameron for your very kind words. I am glad that I was able to help you in a way that best fit your needs. That is always a priority. You were a wonderful and patient client. It was a great pleasure working with you.

reviews.text: Excellent work. Highly recommended.  Olga made sure I understood each step of the process. She explains everything in details, and response to questions quickly.  I'm glad I found a great lawyer. My case is closed and I got what I needed! Thank you Olga!
reviews.ownerResponse: It was a great pleasure working with you Yelena. One of the best parts of my profession is getting to know dedicated, strong, and hardworking parents like you. I wish you all the best.

reviews.text: Olga was amazing during my divorce. She walked me through all the steps and made sure I knew everything that was going to happen and made sure  I was comfortable and well informed. She's amazing and I definitely would recommend her to anyone in need of a family lawyer.

reviews.text: Along with being a great attorney, Olga is a beautiful person. Olga provided my husband Joe and I with professional and excellent service. She is very knowledgeable and was responsive to our questions at all times. Olga positively made the difference for us. I recommend her for any Family Law Services.

reviews.text: The Law Office of Olga Koplik is unique in our community. She is not just an outstanding attorney, she one of the kindest human beings I have met. The most important aspect of any professional (Attorneys, Doctors, etc.) is weather they care about their client - she cares beyond any attorney I have met to date. Unlike many attorneys whose primary goal is to make money from high end clients, her intentions are to help you and your children. These are not just words, but deeds and acts. She has background in psychology, which I think every family attorney should have, which helps with dealing with pathologic family dynamics more effectively. She is extremely trustworthy, honest, responsive (yes, she responds in timely fashion!), fair, patient and knowledgeable. We need more of her in our community.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you Dr. Sahar for trusting me with your matter and for your very kind words.

reviews.text: Refuses to respond to emails and calls, Doesn’t live up to the promises he makes has forgotten multiple times to submit paperwork that he said he would, Impossible to get case updates and know what’s going on when your lawyer dodges you left and right. Only reason why I know what’s going on with my case is I have to ask other lawyers to look into it for me.
Had an intake, they seem unorganized. We’re taking a long time to get back to Mr the. Said they didn’t have time for my case. Terrible firm
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hi Alicia,

I'm sorry about your experience. Shortly after you reached out to our office we were able to set up a comprehensive intake call with you where we spent about 45 minutes going through the details of your situation. After that I reviewed your intake and spoke with our team about your situation. There was a delay in communicating the outcome of our meeting to you. You caught us at a time where we are transitioning to a new system which contributed to the delay. And for that I apologize. I reviewed our communication with you about being unable to assist you and we only communicated that we were not able to assist you. And it appears that's what prompted your feedback.

I did not see any communication where we told you we didn't have the time to take your case. The reasons we have to decline representation can vary based on a number of factors. However, in an effort to be helpful, we provided you with a referral to a law firm that might be able to assist you. I do hope that you're able to find someone to assist you and wish you the best of luck.

If you would like to discuss your experience, please reach out to me directly.

reviews.text: I highly recommend Drew should you find yourself in need of an employment lawyer in California. He is thorough with his work and takes the time to help you understand the legal provisions and considerations in your situation. You won't be disappointed when working with Drew!
Excellent team and lawyer...very helpful...thankyou!

Drew is an absolute master of his trade. It was such a pleasure working with him on my specialized case. His turnaround time was incredibly quick, he was very thorough, and clearly knowledgeable. You can tell his response are always thoughtful and educated. Very grateful I came across his firm. Highly recommend it.
Really Appreciate the time Drew Lewis spent with me informing me and guiding on my best interests for my case would definitely recommend to any one and they reply within a reasonable time frame!
reviews.ownerResponse: It was nice chatting with you. Follow the steps I gave you—I think they’ll help you resolve your issue.

reviews.text: Drew Lewis helped me out when my options were uncomfortable and unclear, and exceeded my expectations both on a personal and professional level. Drew comes with the tenacity and attention to detail that you want in a lawyer, but provides a human and compassionate approach to clients. He thoroughly reviewed my situation, made sure I understand the laws around it and what my options are. I appreciated learning throughout our conversations always keeping me informed.  He provides his services with full transparency, swiftness, and compassion. I recommend Drew Lewis without hesitation.

If I could give Lemon Law Pro 100 stars I would. Long story short I bought a car I thought was bulletproof. Not the case. After multiple times in the shop I found a “lemon law firm” online. They did absolutely nothing for me. I was able to get out of the contract and luckily found Kimberly and LaDawna at Lemon Law Pro. They went to work immediately and I am happy to say after several months (it’s a timely process) the manufacturer has settled with way more than I thought we’d ever see. Do yourself a favor if you have a true lemon. Go to Lemon Law Pro, they are local and know how to get it done.

The owner spent 30+ minutes with me by phone explaining detailed Lemon law and what I can expect from the auto manufacturer.  Since a reasonable offer was already presented to me, the attorney did not believe her services were required. Whats interesting is she already knew she would not accept the referral but still spent time explaining why the offer was presented based on law. This advice was free! God forbid I have another Lemon but if I do, this is my first choice to represent my interest.

We had a great experience working with Lemon Lawyer Pro attorney Vince and team. From the first consultation, they were honest, knowledgeable, and responsive. They explained every step clearly, handled all communication with the manufacturer, and achieved a settlement that exceeded our expectations. We never felt alone in the process truly top notch service! Can't thank them enough!!!

Words can not describe how thankful I am for Lemon Law Pro. I am a Sacramento local and just found them on Google, I didn't want to opt for a big time LA attorney, and I'm glad I didn't. My first time interacting with Lemon Law Pro was in 2022 when I had a brand new Honda I was having an issue with. At that time they were honest in the fact that my case wouldn't have been strong enough and that they had a 100% success rate but didn't feel they could get me anything. I appreciated that honest feedback. I decided to get rid of that car and get a 2023 Honda. Little did I know that this car would have triple the issues. As soon as I was being denied help directly from the manufacturer I contacted Lemon Law Pro again and they immediately took on my case in Jan 2024. Ever since then it took about 5 months to get me a settlement I was absolutely happy with! I can not thank LaDawna enough for all her hard work. To all the paralegals and also Kim Zazzi! If you have any concerns your car might be a lemon, definitely hit up Lemon Law Pro!!!
Update September 2025: They knocked it out of the park AGAIN for me on a 2nd lemon I got sadly. But the team did the wonderful work they do best and go my case settled and closed in about 4 months time. AMAZING WORK :)
I recently had to use their services and I have to say that LaDawna was really knowledgeable, really helpful and made the process super easy. Recommend them 100%, thank you guys for everything!

Even months after my case settled I got into another new car that ran into some problems. No questions asked ....LaDawna, Kim, and her team jumped right back in and gave me the assistance and guidance I needed. If you even think your car could be a lemon these are the folks you want to call. Personable, honest, and communicative. Thanks you guys again!!! <3

Vince and his team fought a long and difficult battle for me in a lemon law case against a Motorcyle manufacturer and secured a settlement in my favor. Vince and his team were always available for questions and kept me informed throughout the case. I would highly recommend giving them a call if you are facing vehicle issues that are not being adequately handled under normal warranty channels. I’m hoping I never have vehicle issues again that require an attorney, but if I do, I will be using Lemon Law Pro again.
Lemon Law Pro far exceeded my expectations with my case. When I had all but forgot and given up thinking I had no case, Kimberli surprised me with a great settlement. Thank you!

Trustworthy and very responsive! After submitting all repair work for my vehicle to the team it was determined that I did not have enough grounds to proceed. While that was a bummer I appreciated the fact that they did not waste my time. It turned out that I was able to have the ongoing issues fixed and Dodge offered good compensation for my troubles outside the Lemon Law option. I was skeptical and had the Lemon Law Pro team review the document. They guided me down a good path, explained their reasoning and made me feel comfortable with my final decision. Thanks Ashley and LaDawna.
Made it a very easy process, explained all my option, felt like they really were really looking out for me. The office would always update me on the status when I’d call!

I reached out to Lemon Law Pro regarding issues I’ve have had with my 2020 Range Rover Sport. I had filed a BBB AutoLine claim against Range Rover but was at a loss as to next steps, the Lemon Law process, how the laws worked and if the manufacturer’s settlement was fair. LaDawna was awesome! She contacted me to go over all my questions and reviewed the settlement agreement that I got from the manufacturer for free! She was knowledgeable, friendly and helpful! If you ever have the unfortunate luck to have to deal with a Lemon, give these folks a call!

We feel fortunate to have found the very competent, responsive Lemon Law Pro office in Roseville to handle our Lemon Law case.

It was disappointing, time consuming, and frustrating that our expensive ($50K), new (2019) minivan was a ‘lemon’ with an engine and transmission that never was reliable. We needed to replace it and its resale value was seriously compromised by the repair order history.  We were also out-of-pocket for losses not covered by warranty. Selling the mini-van back to the manufacturer under terms of the California Lemon Law was the best option but we determined that option was best handled by a lawyer who specialized in lemon law.
Big LA Lemon Law firms rushed us with a hard sell, some individual general law practitioners with no lemon law experience assured us they could help. None of those options felt comfortable.
Happily, we found Lemon Law Pro in Roseville (Sacramento) only 30 miles from our home which was reassuring even though we never had to visit their office in-person. All of our transactions with them, except turning-in the minivan at a dealer, were conducted on-line, including transferring our records to them. The support staff was always readily available for phone, text, and email communication.
Within five months from the date we signed the representation agreement they jumped through all the necessary legal hoops with no drama, to take the minivan off our hands, and get us a settlement large enough to purchase a new 2023 replacement that is entirely satisfactory. It was clear Lemon Law Pro’s well-organized operation, specialized knowledge, experience in Northern California, personal contacts, and reputation made this very efficient transaction possible.
We enthusiastically recommended Lemon Law Pro of Roseville.
Grady Hesters
I have worked with Kimberli and she is an amazing professional.

They were amazing. Plus they got me WAY more $$ than I would have been able to get by doing it myself (I tried). Do not do this on your own, its not worth it. The manufacturer also paid all of the legal fees ontop of my settlement.

I would like to thank LemonLawPros Kimberli Zazzi, Vincent Onorio and staff for professionally handling our lemon law case and getting the case concluded in a quick manner.
Thanks Melody

The team at Lemon Law Pro was excellent! They made the entire process very easy. They kept in constant contact and answered any and all questions we had, in a very timely manner. They were able to secure a settlement with Ford motor company, at a better than thought possible amount. We would have never been able to achieve this without Vince and his team.

I high recommend Lemon Law Pro:

Purchased a NEW 2021 Audi Q5 from Audi Rocklin (CA) within 1 week of purchase we had problems with the MMI Screen (Multi-Media Interface); then started having problems with the Key Fob, the car would NOT start even with the Key Fob in the emergency start position.  With over 30+ trips to the service department and trying to work with Audi Rocklin and Audi USA we  were offered $5,000 to shut up and go away.  That's when we contacted Lemon Law Pro and it was the best decision we ever made!

Vince, Bernadette & Talerra worked with us for a year (5/20/22 to 5/19/23) and it was worth the wait!  We got almost all of our money back thanks to at Lemon Law Pro.

It's a very frustrating and lengthily process but I guarantee that you will be satisfied with the end results.

BIG shout out to Vince, Bernadette, Talerra and the entire Lemon Law Pro Staff.

Such an easy and pleasant team to work with. LaDawna and her team were always communicative and responsive throughout the entire process of my case and I could not be happier. Glad to have been able to work with such an enthusiastic and encouraging group for my lemon. Would highly recommend if anyone is in need of a group of professionals to look after and oversee their legal case.

Thank you again Lemon Law Pro!
I heard about lemon law and had come across Lemon Law Pro regarding the problems that I was having with my vehicle. They were able to evaluate, process and close my case in a timely manner.
My experience with lemon law pro was phenomenal. Gnader and Vince made the whole process a breeze, I would 100% recommend them to anyone that has a potential lemon for a car.

LaDawna and her staff were amazing and answered all my questions regarding my lemon law case. Was able to get the result I wanted. Would definitely recommend them and will be going through them again if I have any issues with any of my newer cars!

Basically the same situation as everyone else, bought a new 2018 RAM 1500 Laramie which since new had a minor but chronic electronics glitch that the dealership was unable to isolate and was not able to repair.  The pickup was out of warranty when I finally involved Lemon Law Pro.  It’s a somewhat lengthy and contorted process dealing with Fiat Chrysler but the Law Firm handled my case deftly and courteously, resolving the issue professionally with zero hassles on my part.  I would highly recommend Lemon Law Pro to anyone needing legal intervention regarding a defective vehicle or problems with a manufacturer.

I bought a brand new 2022 Dodge Ram 2500 and after a few months noticed it kept leaking oil out the bottom of the engine. After a couple failed attempts at the dealership to fix the problem I knew I had a problem. I googled lemon law and I came upon Lemon Law Pro. They had the highest stars and and I didn’t know anything about lemon law so I gave them a call.

They were so professional and straight forward about what the process looked like. They were able to completely resolve my issue and I was able to return the truck and and be properly reimbursed. They have the most polite staff and truly felt like they empathized with my situation and fought hard on my behalf to correct the issue. I strongly recommend, and have to friends, their expertise. At no point did I feel like I was dealing with just an attorney who wants to rush the process but rather a friend who was there ti help me. They deserve 10 stars but Google only has 5. A big thank you goes out to them for their great work!!

They were incredibly helpful and explained things in a way that made it easy to understand. I felt like they genuinely cared about my case and fought hard for a great settlement. I highly recommend them!

The receptionist answered the phone so upset. This law firm should definitely talk to her about her customer service if they care for their reputation. She was arguing with me about my case. I told her she’s very unprofessional to be talking like that and she yelled “you too” and hung up. I wouldn’t recommend a law firm whose receptionist is speaking on behalf of the attorney. That says a lot about their services and the feedback you’ll get.

I bought a used Audi in 2021, but the camera has been having problems and has not been fixed many times. I contacted lemon law pro ,they are very responsible, told me to prepare the information to them, will help me responsible for the end. Unfortunately, the law changed in 2022, and the law no longer protects used car owners, so I can't return my car to Audi. But they helped me get a good compensation and helped me recover some of my losses. I really appreciate them.

I heard about lemon law and had come across Lemon Law Pro regarding the problems that I was having with my 2020 GMC Sierra 1500. They were able to evaluate, process and close my case in a timely manner.
What a great experience! Everyone was so helpful and communicated the process so I’d know what to expect. I’d highly recommend working the Lemon Law Pro team.

Got referred over by a friend said they do good work! But when i called Kelly Robinson the lady who answers the phone is really rude ! If you don’t like your job find a new one. Learn how to talk to people w/o an attitude

I just recently contacted Lemon Law Pro and they were awesome! Their team is super responsive, Friendly and informative. It is nice to feel like you have someone that is on your team and is representing you in the best possible way! Highly recommend.

I contacted Lemon Law Pro in early 2021 about our 2020 Ford Explorer with too many issues to list.  From the start everyone I had contact with was professional and informative.  If I had questions they always provided prompt answers.  We ended up with a buyback offer from Ford that was fair and reasonable.  If you think you may not have a case call Lemon Law Pro – they are clearly experts in this area and will provide you with guidance on how to move forward.

Can't say enough good things about our experience.

We were thinking we had no case, Lemon Law encouraged us to go forward and we ended up with a substantial settlement with no work on our part except signing the paperwork.

Couldn't be happier with the results.
Excellent company

I had a Kia Sportage that ran fine until the last 6 months of the lease. Then it was in the shop like every week for transmission problems. The third visit they kept it for over 6 weeks and neither communicated with me nor offered me a loaner.
I was getting SO fed up and just didn't know what to do. I had already contacted Kia customer service and was getting some help there, but nowhere near enough.
A VERY dear friend referred me to Lemon Law Pro on the off-chance they could help. WOW. They spoke directly with Kia on my behalf and took all those challenges off my plate. They somehow got through to them to give me a loaner free of charge while they attempted to fix the tranni, which simply should have just been replaced instead of giving a new widow a runaround. AND, they got me a cash settlement twice the amount that Kia offered me.
They were kind, patient, understanding, comprehensive of the situation I found myself in, and more than willing to go to bat for me. I can't say enough good about them. If you even THINK your car is a "lemon", contact them. They'll tell you straight up if they can help. Thanks so much to all the pros at Lemon Law Pro.

When I had a serious issue with my car, I called Lemon Law Pro. They were amazing. They walked me through the entire process. They were very professional and communicated quickly when necessary.  My case was resolved far better than I could have imagined.  I would use them again in a heartbeat.

We purchased a 2018 Ford F150 with the 5.0 engine that has needed an engine replacement, two new dashboards due to warping, has excessive knocking/engine issues and suffers from excessive oil consumption. After contacting Talerra at Lemon Law Pros we e-mailed her our registration and repair records for review. After no response for over two weeks I called her and she apologized and said she was out for a medical issue and someone else was handling our case. Furthermore, she said Ford had agreed to buy back our truck for $35,000 dollars and we should be receiving that offer shortly. I reported this info to my wife as she was handling the e-mails. She e-mailed Talerra who responded that she had only received our registration and one page of service records. My wife confirmed that she sent a file with 51 pages of repair records, TSBs, etc. Apparently Lemon Law Pros has a glitch in their system and cannot open the file and still claims they haven't received it. We confirmed it was sent a second time. No phone call from them, no concern. Talerra obviously got our case confused with another client as we have no offer and are back at square one. Time to find new representation and a competent company that will handle our "lemon." Extremely dissatisfied.

We contacted Lemon Law Pro several months ago about our F-250 which had been in the shop for multiple things and occasions, mostly for electrical issues.  After submitting our paperwork, we were called by one of the attorneys and had a nice conversation, after which she said she was taking our case.  Because of COVID there was a bit of a delay in getting thru the courts part, but once that happened everything cruised on along.  At all times Lemon Law Pro kept us up to date and no question was too silly for us to ask.  They always answered quickly and professionally.  Everyone we spoke to was extremely helpful and friendly!  Which we really appreciated.  Thank you Lemon Law Pro for taking our lemon out of our life!! And being so darn nice!!

I had a vehicle that was eligible for Lemon Law buyback. It was taking an extraordinary long time so I reached out to Lemon Law Pro for assistance. They asked me questions regarding the buyback, the status, and gave me insightful feedback regarding interaction with the manufacturer. Lemon Law Pro also told me to send the buyback offer from the manufacturer and they would review as a courtesy. They gave me realistic guidance during a confusing and long process. Super professional, incredibly helpful!

I bought a new 2015 Ram Laramie EcoDiesel (which I loved by the way) but started having major problems within the first 10,000 miles and continued to have major issues until I was done! My truck was in and out of the dealer repair shop many times which added up to over 180 days!  After 3+ years of this I decided to contact Lemon Pro and they were absolutely wonderful to work with. I was amazed at how quickly they got a factory re-purchase claim for me. I would recommend them any time. Brandon and his team were especially great to work with and always responded quickly to my many emails.
I never received a response from this organization.

I was having issues with my 2020 Sierra and the dealership no longer wanted to help and got nowhere with GMC. I thought I was s.o.l. But after doing some research I found Lemon Law Pros and god I am so glad I did!!! Attorney Kimberli called me and after discussing my case she decided to take on it. Everyone at the office was profesional, kind and knowledgeable, I mostly worked with Dulce and she was fantastic !! They took care of everything got me more than I expected. 100% recommend them if you have a lemon!!!

The staff at Lemon Law pro made the experiance easy to understand, helped me every step of the way and fought for a fair deal. While it should have been an easy win, the manufacturer fought us every step of the way. Lemon Law continued to push and in the end we got a great settlement. They stuck by us through almost a 2 year battle! During the waiting period, they kept me updated and made sure I knew what was happening and why. I appreciate everything they did for us!
If you need legal help with your lemon, this is the team for you! They are honest, reliable, persistent and knowledgeable.

If I could give them a 10 star review I would!!!!

I’ll start by saying I would give zero stars if it were an option. Amerio Law, Ashley Injury Lawyers, whatever they are calling themselves these days are the epitome of why attorneys are so disliked. They agreed to take my personal injury case on contingency 3½ years ago even though they were probably not qualified to handle what essentially was a medical malpractice case. My guess is they were hurting for revenue during the height of Covid. I am not even sure they had an attorney to work the case until last year. They puttered around and kept me in the dark for the most part and suddenly called me and said as a matter of previously overlooked housekeeping chores, my wife and I needed to sign electronic substitution of attorney forms and we trustingly did so. The very next day, I received another call telling me they were dropping the case for business reasons. I now know that having us sign the substitution of attorney forms under false pretense was so they would not have to go back and explain to The Court why they were dropping the case. Basically, they made it look like we fired them! To add insult to injury, they quickly filed a lien against any future settlement proceeds making it virtually impossible to find another attorney to accept the case. My advice is to stay as far away from this law firm as possible!

The very worst experience with this attorney and the firm. The paralegal or what ever Donna was would never allow me to speak to my Ashley at all when I had questions about my case. I then said if my calls were not returned I would be more than happy to come wait in the office so I could exercise my rights as a client of this firm and speak to Ashley. I then got a phone call stating they were withdrawing as my attorney and there would be an attorney lien on my claim. They made me do all the footwork and then they with draw and still want to be paid? WOW!!! If you’re ever in a crash or anything else… never call this firm. Read all the yelp and reviews on how horrible they are. Wish I would have. Just found out that the PL she had working my case is now suing her for wrongful termination.

Dont even bother getting a lawyer at this point. I ended up with less money than i started with. Crazy.

Do yourself a favor.  Before wasting time with this firm — read their AVVO reviews (average 4 out of 10). Also note, they are not “Super Lawyers.” Also note, most of the google reviews are fake.  Finally note that Brian Clark’s average tenure at a law firm is a couple years before changing the name of the firm.

If you hire ARG, no attorney will ever come to a phone - ever - not once and all estate planning and legal paperwork will be handled by a young and inexperienced real estate broker downloading documents downloading documents off the internet.
reviews.ownerResponse:
Hello Tully, respectfully we do not have a 4/10 Avvo score. Avvo scores aren't graded out of 10, they are out of 5. Brian Clark has a 3.6/5, nearly double what you claim.

Regarding Brian Clark's tenure, he has only been with two firms in the past 10 years (see LinkedIn).

The name of this firm has only changed one time and that was completed 3.5 years ago.

Regarding the paperwork, almost all law firms have staff they use who are not licensed attorneys, it is common practice.

The reviews are not fake, and if you go to SuperLawyers.com and type in Attorneys Real Estate Group, you'll see our attorney Robert J. Enos is, in fact a Super Lawyer.

We're sorry you had a bad experience but your claims are simply not accurate.

So of course when I got an eviction notice and tried contacting him he was no where to be found. The bank was trying to come up with some bs about things I don’t even know and tried kicking us to the street. Thanks to these guys research and expertise I was not held responsive, not kicked to the street and received compensation for my troubles. Don’t know what they did but their countless efforts, dedication, and experience won my case. Five starts and thumbs up!!!
reviews.ownerResponse: Hey Ryan, thank you for taking the time to share your experience and for the five-star review. We are so sorry that you had to face such a distressing situation with your landlord, but are relieved and pleased to know that our team was able to effectively resolve the issue for you.
